ctx.scope:
6.4.1	Office Building and Structural Interventions
The project goes beyond a standard aesthetic upgrade, acting as a comprehensive repositioning to new-build equivalent standards.
•	The main air-rights building will undergo a deep retro-fit, while the Villiers Street entrance building will be completely demolished and rebuilt.
•	Structural modifications will vertically extend the massing of the Villiers Street building.
•	Existing terraces on the south elevation will be modified to improve accessibility, and upper roof levels will be altered to increase Net Internal Area and external amenity spaces.
•	The building envelope, specifically the facade and roof, will see comprehensive refurbishment to replace aging fabric and improve thermal performance.
•	Inside, floor plates will be enhanced to maximise natural light and create stimulating work settings.
•	The project includes best-in-class end-of-journey facilities, featuring cycle storage, dedicated cycle lifts, showers, and lockers.
•	Outdated building services will be replaced with new, highly efficient heating, cooling, and ventilation plant systems to drastically lower operational carbon.
6.4.2	Retail Strategy and Enhancements
The retail footprint will be revitalised and expanded, targeting an increase to 48,000 square feet of Net Lettable Area.
•	The works will renew the retail offering beneath the Embankment Place underpass, within the vaults on Villiers Street, and throughout The Arches.
•	Standard retail units will be delivered as 'white-box' shells with high-quality facades.
•	Units located within the historic railway arches will be treated differently; original brickwork will be carefully cleaned, repaired, and treated to ensure structural integrity while showcasing the authentic architectural patina.
•	The overall strategy aims to improve street visibility, offer flexible unit sizes, and maximise signage to boost all-day footfall from Hungerford Bridge.
6.4.3	Public Realm and Estate Transformation
The scope extends beyond the immediate building footprint to create a cohesive estate identity, though interventions outside the red line boundary are subject to planning approval.
•	Embankment Gardens and Villiers Street South: A new arrival square will be created to orientate pedestrians. The boundary with Victoria Embankment Gardens will be reshaped, exploring the removal of raised steps to improve flow, and the bandstand will be reinvented to provide a new frontage facing Villiers Street.
•	Charing Cross Station Forecourt: The current vehicle-dominated space will be transformed to prioritise pedestrians. The monument will be reframed by a new pocket garden featuring kiosks and informal seating.
•	Embankment Place Underpass: The route connecting Villiers Street to Northumberland Avenue will receive a new paving language, lighting, and planting to compliment the new retail and tunnel soffit treatments.
•	The Concourse: The currently poorly lit, transient space connecting the station to Hungerford Bridge will be turned into a generous, inviting route with places to pause.
ctx.assumptions: The office upgrades works is being planned for a speculative build as the current tenants lease will soon be expiring

1.desc: CCTV covering all vehicle approaches and perimeter; Dedicated security monitoring in secure area

critDescs.disruption: Level of impact on day-to-day activities and business operations
critDescs.fatalities: Potential for injuries, casualties or loss of life
critDescs.reputation: Effect on stakeholder confidence, brand, and public perception
critDescs.financial: Direct and indirect financial losses including remediation costs
critDescs.property: Physical damage to buildings, infrastructure, and assets
critDescs.visibility: Public profile, branding, and prominence of the site
critDescs.status: Association with prominent corporate, political, or religious entities
critDescs.access: Ease with which threat actors can access the site and its assets
critDescs.collateral: Proximity to higher-profile or iconic targets nearby
critDescs.crime: Defined crime patterns and trends in the local area
critDescs.history: History of the site or similar sites being targeted
critDescs.vuln: Overall susceptibility of the site to the identified threat
critDescs.opImpact: Effect on operations if this asset is compromised
critDescs.dependencies: Degree to which other assets/systems depend on this asset
critDescs.impactPeople: Consequences for people if this asset is compromised
critDescs.impactEnv: Environmental consequences if this asset is compromised
critDescs.effectiveness: How well the existing control reduces the threat
critDescs.confidence: How proven, tested, and reliable the control is

fatalities.4: Loss of life possible, multiple injuries

reputation.5: Complete loss of confidence, sustained media

access.1: Strictly controlled, very hard to reach

crime.5: Major crime issue, serious/violent crime present

vuln.5: Little or no resistance, highly vulnerable

dependencies.3: Some systems depend on this asset
dependencies.4: Many systems critically depend on this
dependencies.5: Total dependency, single point of failure

impactPeople.5: Severe harm, potential loss of life

capability.1: No known capability — no resources, expertise, or materials available to threat actors locally
capability.2: Limited capability — minimal resources, rudimentary expertise, significant barriers to execution
capability.3: Moderate capability — some resources and expertise exist locally/regionally, attack feasible with effort
capability.4: Strong capability — resources, expertise, and materials readily available, proven attack methods exist
capability.5: Very strong capability — sophisticated resources, extensive expertise, materials easily obtained, attacks executed elsewhere

intent.1: No known intent — no evidence of motivation, no ideological/financial drivers, site has no symbolic value
intent.2: Low intent — minimal motivation, site not a preferred target type, no intelligence indicating interest
intent.3: Moderate intent — some motivation exists, site type has been targeted elsewhere, general threat group activity in region
intent.4: High intent — clear motivation, site type actively targeted, intelligence suggests interest in similar targets
intent.5: Very high intent — strong ideological/financial drivers, specific intelligence, site type is a priority target for active groups

threat.5: Very High — multiple sources, readily available

areaCriteria.label: Local Roads & Access
areaCriteria.prompt: Describe the local road network, major routes (A-roads, motorways), access points, and any traffic considerations within half a mile

areaCriteria.label: Building & Park Adjacencies
areaCriteria.prompt: Describe adjacent buildings, land uses, parks, open spaces, and neighbouring properties within half a mile

areaCriteria.label: Local Transport Hubs
areaCriteria.prompt: Identify railway stations, bus stations, underground/metro stops, cycle hire docks, and taxi ranks within half a mile

areaCriteria.label: Local Landmarks & Points of Interest
areaCriteria.prompt: Identify notable landmarks, places of worship, government buildings, embassies, monuments, and tourist attractions within half a mile

areaCriteria.label: Historic Crime & Terrorism Incidents
areaCriteria.prompt: Describe any significant crime incidents, terrorism events, protests, public disorder, or security incidents that have occurred within half a mile in the past 5 years

areaCriteria.label: Planned Future Developments
areaCriteria.prompt: Identify any planned or ongoing major construction, regeneration schemes, transport projects, or significant developments within half a mile, excluding the subject project

areaAnalysis.roads: 1 Embankment Place is bounded by Villiers Street to the west, a heavily pedestrianised route, and Victoria Embankment (A3211) to the south, a major arterial road along the River Thames. The Strand (A4) lies just to the north, serving as a primary east-west traffic and bus corridor. The site sits within the London Congestion Charge and Ultra Low Emission Zones, with vehicular access and loading tightly constrained by high pedestrian footfall.
areaAnalysis.adjacencies: The subject property is an air-rights development built directly over Charing Cross Railway Station, which forms its primary structural adjacency. To the immediate east lies Victoria Embankment Gardens, a public park that attracts significant daytime foot traffic. The surrounding half-mile radius is densely developed with mixed-use commercial offices, retail, hospitality venues, and the pedestrian walkways of the Golden Jubilee Bridges.
areaAnalysis.transport: The site benefits from exceptional public transport links, sitting directly above Charing Cross Station (National Rail) and adjacent to Embankment Underground Station (Bakerloo, Northern, Circle, and District lines). Numerous high-frequency bus routes operate along the Strand (A4) less than 0.1 miles away. Additionally, Embankment Pier offers river bus services, and several Santander Cycles hire docking stations are located within a five-minute walk.
areaAnalysis.landmarks: Trafalgar Square, a major tourist destination and frequent gathering point, is located approximately 0.2 miles to the northwest. Key government buildings and ministries along Whitehall are situated roughly 0.3 miles to the southwest. Other notable points of interest within a half-mile include the National Gallery, the Savoy Hotel, and Cleopatra's Needle on the Victoria Embankment.
areaAnalysis.crime_history: The immediate vicinity experiences high volumes of petty crime, particularly pickpocketing and mobile phone snatching, driven by the dense tourist and commuter populations around Charing Cross. Trafalgar Square and Whitehall, both within half a mile, are historic and regular focal points for large-scale protests, including environmental and political demonstrations that occasionally cause public disorder. While no major terrorist attacks have occurred at this specific address in the past five years, the area's prominence and proximity to government infrastructure mean it remains at a high theoretical risk in line with the broader UK threat level.
areaAnalysis.developments: The surrounding Westminster and North Bank areas are subject to continuous commercial refurbishments and public realm improvements, such as the recently completed Strand Aldwych pedestrianisation to the northeast. Across the River Thames, within a half-mile radius, the redevelopment of 72 Upper Ground (former ITV Studios) into a massive commercial complex is a significant ongoing project. Excluding the subject site's own planned major retrofit, future local developments primarily consist of high-end office modernisations and sustainability upgrades.

VBIED.desc: Vehicle-borne — typically 100-2000kg depending on vehicle type

IED-Drone.desc: Drone-delivered — typically 1-10kg payload limited by drone capacity

Mail/Parcel Threat.desc: Postal device — typically 0.1-2kg limited by packaging

threatDB.label: VBIED / VAAW
threatDB.description: Vehicle-borne improvised explosive device (VBIED) or vehicle used as a weapon (VAAW). Encompasses car/van bombs and deliberate vehicle ramming attacks against people or structures.

threatDB.label: Marauding Terrorist Attack (MTA / MTFA)
threatDB.description: Coordinated attack by one or more individuals using bladed weapons, firearms, or improvised weapons to cause mass casualties while moving through a location.

threatDB.label: PBIED
threatDB.description: Person-borne improvised explosive device — a concealed explosive device carried or worn by an attacker, typically detonated in a crowded space.

threatDB.label: IED / Drone / Mail Threat
threatDB.description: Improvised explosive devices placed or delivered by hand, drone, or postal/courier service. Includes suspect packages and small unmanned aerial system (UAS) threats.

threatDB.label: CBR / CBRN Attack
threatDB.description: Chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear attack using toxic agents, pathogens, or radioactive material to cause harm, contamination, or mass disruption.

threatDB.label: Burglary
threatDB.description: Unlawful entry into premises with intent to steal, commit criminal damage, or cause harm. Includes forced entry, opportunistic access, and distraction techniques.

threatDB.label: Theft
threatDB.description: Deprivation of assets without consent. Includes shoplifting, employee theft, and theft of high-value or CRAVED (Concealable, Removable, Available, Valuable, Enjoyable, Disposable) items.

threatDB.label: Vehicle Crime
threatDB.description: Theft of vehicles, theft from vehicles, or criminal damage to vehicles. Includes carjacking, catalytic converter theft, and fuel theft.

threatDB.label: Arson
threatDB.description: Deliberate ignition of fire to destroy property, cause harm, or create disruption. May be opportunistic or targeted.

threatDB.label: Trespass
threatDB.description: Unauthorised entry onto land or into buildings without permission. Ranges from opportunistic to deliberate (protest, reconnaissance, squatting).

threatDB.label: ASB / Criminal Damage
threatDB.description: Anti-social behaviour, nuisance, intimidation, and deliberate damage to property. May escalate or create secondary risks.

threatDB.label: Public Order / Protest
threatDB.description: Protests, demonstrations, riots, or direct action that may disrupt operations, damage property, or endanger people.

threatDB.label: Violence / Sexual Offences
threatDB.description: Physical assault, domestic violence, threats of violence, and sexual offences on or near the premises.

threatDB.label: Cybercrime
threatDB.description: Attacks on IT, OT, and IoT systems including ransomware, phishing, DDoS, network intrusion, and operational technology compromise.

threatDB.label: Information Theft
threatDB.description: Theft or unauthorised disclosure of confidential, commercially sensitive, or personal data. Includes document theft, covert photography, and data exfiltration.

threatDB.label: Insider Threat
threatDB.description: Malicious or negligent actions by employees, contractors, or trusted individuals with authorised access. Includes sabotage, fraud, and data theft.

threatDB.label: Serious Organised Crime
threatDB.description: Organised criminal activity including drug supply, human trafficking, money laundering, and professional theft gangs targeting premises.

threatDB.label: Kidnap, Ransom & Extortion
threatDB.description: Threats to individuals or organisations including kidnapping, ransom demands, product contamination threats, and extortion via digital or physical means.

treatmentDB.label: VBIED / VAAW
treatmentDB.narrative: The primary external treatment is establishing and enforcing vehicle standoff distance through hostile vehicle mitigation. The building perimeter must prevent unauthorised vehicle approach to the shell. Internal blast-resilient construction and laminated glazing reduce the consequence of detonation.
ext_physical: Hostile Vehicle Mitigation (HVM) — bollards, planters or rated barriers at the perimeter to enforce standoff distance
Anti-ram rated barriers at all vehicular entry points integrated with access control
Speed reduction measures on approach — chicanes, raised tables, surface changes
Vehicle search/inspection area at safe standoff with overwatch position
Reinforced collision/blast jackets to structural columns within vehicle reach
Laminated anti-blast glazing to facades facing vehicle approaches
Window film or sacrificial glazing to reduce fragmentation
ext_electronic: ANPR at vehicle access points for identification and audit
CCTV covering all vehicle approaches and perimeter
int_physical: Blast-resilient construction to reception and entrance lobbies
procedures: Blast analysis and standoff distance modelling to inform perimeter layout

treatmentDB.label: MTA / MTFA
treatmentDB.narrative: Marauding attack mitigation requires layered defences: early detection through surveillance outside the building, denial of entry through screening and access control at the threshold, internal compartmentalisation to limit attacker movement inside the building, and rehearsed lockdown procedures to protect occupants.
ext_physical: Clear sight lines across all external areas for early detection of hostile approach
Removal of concealment opportunities in landscaping, furniture and recessed areas
Hostile Vehicle Mitigation if the threat involves vehicle approach
ext_electronic: External CCTV with behavioural analytics for loitering and hostile reconnaissance detection
int_physical: Internal compartmentalisation — security-rated doors enabling zones to be isolated during an attack
Offices and meeting rooms with thumb-turn lockable doors for immediate shelter
int_electronic: Lockdown capability activated from reception, security control room and distributed panic buttons
Weapons detection screening at main entry points
Internal CCTV at all corridors, stair cores and lift lobbies for real-time tracking
procedures: Invacuation routes and designated safe havens within the building core
Lockdown procedures with regular rehearsal and testing programme
Hostile reconnaissance awareness training for all building users
Collaboration with Counter Terrorism Security Advisors (CTSA)
Communication cascade for rapid alert during an incident
SIA-licensed security operatives during occupied hours
Emergency communication system linking security, management and emergency services

treatmentDB.label: PBIED
treatmentDB.narrative: Person-borne IED mitigation focuses on detection at entry through screening and search, supported by personnel trained to identify suspicious behaviour. Internal blast resilience reduces the consequence if a device enters the building.
ext_physical: Clear external areas with minimal concealment for device placement
Search and screening regime at building entry points
ext_electronic: Hostile reconnaissance detection through CCTV analytics and trained security personnel
int_physical: Blast-resilient construction to reception and entrance lobby
Laminated glazing to entrance areas to reduce fragmentation
int_electronic: Bag search policy and/or weapons/explosives detection at main entrances
Internal CCTV coverage of all public-facing areas and circulation
Visitor management — identification, sign-in, temporary pass, escort policy
procedures: Staff trained to identify suspicious behaviour and unattended items
Suspicious item/package response procedures
Evacuation and invacuation drills specific to blast scenarios

treatmentDB.label: IED / Drone / Mail
treatmentDB.narrative: IED and postal threat treatment isolates incoming mail and deliveries from occupied areas with dedicated screening facilities.
ext_physical: Perimeter security preventing unauthorised access to building shell and service areas
ext_electronic: CCTV analytics for detection of suspicious items and unattended packages
Counter-UAS detection and mitigation measures where drone delivery risk is assessed
Monitoring of airspace above site for unauthorised drone activity
int_physical: Dedicated mail/post room physically separated from occupied areas
Receiving room with independent ventilation
Secure delivery holding area for parcels awaiting screening
int_electronic: Mail screening equipment (X-ray) appropriate to threat level
procedures: Mail screening procedures and trained personnel
Suspicious mail identification training for all staff handling deliveries
Delivery vehicle inspection and verification protocols
Response procedures for suspect packages

treatmentDB.label: CBR
treatmentDB.narrative: CBR treatment protects building occupants through secure air handling, detection at intake points, and rapid isolation.
ext_physical: Air intakes elevated and protected from ground-level interference
Perimeter controls preventing access to MEP infrastructure
ext_electronic: CCTV coverage of building air intakes and ventilation access points
int_physical: Sealed post room with independent ventilation
int_electronic: HVAC zoning with rapid isolation and shutdown capability
CBR detection at air handling units where threat assessment requires
procedures: CBR incident response procedures including shelter-in-place
Staff awareness training on recognising CBR indicators
Emergency services liaison on CBR response protocols

treatmentDB.label: Burglary
treatmentDB.narrative: Burglary treatment hardens the building shell outside through perimeter security and lighting, and inside through security-rated doors, windows, glazing and electronic detection to deter, delay and detect forced entry.
ext_physical: Security-rated perimeter fencing/walls at consistent height
Security-rated pedestrian boundary gates with self-closing mechanism
Dusk-to-dawn external lighting at all entries, service areas and parking
Defensive planting beneath windows and along vulnerable boundaries
Landscape maintained for sight lines — canopies above 2.2m, shrubs below 1m
ext_electronic: External CCTV covering perimeter, entries, recessed areas and service bays
int_physical: Security-tested external doorsets with multi-point locking and access control integration
Enhanced security glazing to all ground floor and accessible windows
Laminated glass with internal beading preventing external removal
Window opening restrictors on accessible windows
Fire escape doors alarmed, without external ironmongery
int_electronic: Electronic access control at all external entry points with audit trail
Intruder alarm system with panic buttons and 24/7 monitoring
Door contacts with forced/left-open/tamper alarms on perimeter doors

treatmentDB.label: Theft
treatmentDB.narrative: Theft treatment combines external surveillance and secure cycle parking outside the building, with access control and CCTV inside to remove opportunity.
ext_physical: Cycle stands to police-preferred specification in secure, lit, CCTV-covered enclosures
E-bike/e-scooter storage in fire-rated enclosure separated from building
int_physical: Secure storage for high-value portable equipment
int_electronic: CCTV in stair cores, lift lobbies and circulation areas
Access control preventing unauthorised floor-to-floor movement

treatmentDB.label: Vehicle Crime
treatmentDB.narrative: Vehicle crime treatment relies on surveillance, lighting and natural oversight of parking areas outside the building.
ext_physical: Well-lit parking with uniform illumination and no dark spots
Natural surveillance from occupied rooms into parking areas
ext_electronic: Car park CCTV at all entry/exit points, aisles and pedestrian routes

treatmentDB.label: Arson
treatmentDB.narrative: Arson treatment removes opportunity outside by securing combustible materials away from the building, and inside through comprehensive fire detection, suppression and compartmentation.
ext_physical: Refuse/bin storage lockable and positioned away from building exterior
External lighting to rear and service areas
No combustible material stored against building exterior walls
int_physical: Fire detection system covering all areas including voids and risers
Sprinkler or suppression system
Fire compartmentation to limit spread
Smoke ventilation to protect escape routes
Letterbox fire protection where letter slots exist

treatmentDB.label: Trespass
treatmentDB.narrative: Trespass treatment establishes clear territorial boundaries outside and layered access control inside to prevent unauthorised presence.
ext_physical: Perimeter demarcation through fencing, walls, landscaping and signage
Consistent perimeter boundary height
Clear 2m zone either side of boundary for surveillance
ext_electronic: CCTV with out-of-hours intrusion detection analytics
int_electronic: Electronic access control preventing unauthorised internal movement
Stair core and lift lobby access control at each level

treatmentDB.label: ASB / Criminal Damage
treatmentDB.narrative: ASB and criminal damage treatment applies CPTED principles outside the building — surveillance, lighting and maintenance creating a well-managed environment that discourages antisocial behaviour.
ext_physical: CPTED principles — natural surveillance, defined ownership, active maintenance regime
Robust, securely fixed street furniture and hard landscaping
Anti-graffiti coating on vulnerable external surfaces
Dusk-to-dawn lighting with vandal-resistant fittings at height
ext_electronic: External CCTV with recording for evidence and deterrence

treatmentDB.label: Public Order
treatmentDB.narrative: Public order treatment combines physical crowd management measures outside the building with operational planning for escalation and continuity.
ext_physical: HVM measures that also control crowd movement
Clear pedestrian routes and defined public/private boundaries
ext_electronic: External CCTV with wide-area coverage and recording
procedures: Public order response plan coordinated with police
Escalation procedures for protest or demonstration activity
Business continuity plan for access disruption

treatmentDB.label: Violence / Sexual Offences
treatmentDB.narrative: Violence treatment eliminates concealed, unlit spaces outside where offending could occur, and provides panic alarms, access control and CCTV coverage inside the building.
ext_physical: Well-lit external areas with no concealment opportunities
Natural surveillance from occupied spaces to external areas
ext_electronic: CCTV covering all pedestrian routes, entrances and car parks
int_electronic: Internal CCTV in corridors, stair cores and isolated areas
Panic buttons at reception and public-facing positions
Controlled access preventing unauthorised individuals reaching occupied areas
procedures: Staff trained on conflict de-escalation and personal safety
Lone worker policy and monitoring procedures
Incident recording and pattern analysis

treatmentDB.label: Cybercrime
treatmentDB.narrative: Cybercrime treatment protects digital assets through network architecture, monitoring and staff awareness. Physical security of IT spaces inside the building supports the electronic defences.
int_physical: Security-rated doorsets to server rooms, MER/SER and data storage
int_electronic: Access control to IT infrastructure with full audit trail
Environmental monitoring in server rooms
Network segmentation isolating security, BMS and corporate networks
Intrusion detection and prevention systems
Encrypted storage and transmission for sensitive data
Endpoint protection with managed detection and response
Regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments
Patch management for all systems including IoT and OT
Mobile device management and removable media controls
procedures: Staff cyber awareness training with phishing simulations
Cyber incident response plan
Backup and disaster recovery tested regularly

treatmentDB.label: Information Theft
treatmentDB.narrative: Information theft treatment combines physical security of storage areas inside the building with data handling policies, encryption and monitoring operationally.
int_physical: Security-rated doorsets to areas storing/processing sensitive information
Clear desk policy with secure document storage
Secure print-release preventing uncollected documents
Shredding facilities for confidential waste
int_electronic: Access logging with anomaly detection
Data loss prevention on email and file transfer
procedures: Data classification and handling policy
Encrypted storage and transmission
Secure media disposal procedures
Third-party data processing agreements
Regular information asset audit

treatmentDB.label: Insider Threat
treatmentDB.narrative: Insider threat treatment applies least privilege inside the building through access zoning and monitoring, supported by vetting, behavioural detection and reporting mechanisms operationally.
int_physical: Key management via secure electronic cabinet with audit
int_electronic: Access control zoning restricting individuals to role-required areas only
CCTV in sensitive areas with recording
procedures: Pre-employment vetting and ongoing security screening
Joiners/movers/leavers access process — immediate revocation on departure
Whistleblowing and confidential reporting policy
Regular access rights reviews enforcing least privilege
User behaviour monitoring and anomaly detection
Clear desk and screen policy enforcement
Security clearance for sensitive roles
Separation of duties for critical processes

treatmentDB.label: Serious Organised Crime
treatmentDB.narrative: Serious organised crime treatment addresses vulnerabilities through physical controls inside the building and due diligence, monitoring and fraud prevention operationally.
int_physical: Security-rated doorsets to high-value areas
int_electronic: CCTV at all access points to valuable assets and cash handling
procedures: Background screening for staff and contractors in positions of trust
Supply chain due diligence and third-party risk assessment
Fraud prevention controls with segregation of duties
Anomaly detection in financial and access systems
Organised crime threat briefings
Whistleblowing and confidential reporting

treatmentDB.label: Kidnap, Ransom & Extortion
treatmentDB.narrative: Kidnap, ransom and extortion treatment protects at-risk individuals inside the building through access control and surveillance, with specialist response planning operationally.
int_physical: Controlled access to areas occupied by at-risk individuals
int_electronic: CCTV at entrances capturing identification-quality images
procedures: Travel security procedures for at-risk personnel
Personal security risk assessments for key individuals
Secure communication channels
Kidnap/ransom/extortion incident response plan
Close liaison with police and specialist advisors
